name: Disable SysMain (Superfetch)
description: Stops the SysMain service that preloads app data into RAM. Beneficial on SSDs where it adds unnecessary I/O.

name: Set High Performance Power Plan
description: Switches to the High Performance power plan — CPU never throttles.

name: Enable Ultimate Performance Power Plan
description: Unlocks and activates the hidden Ultimate Performance plan. Maximum CPU/memory throughput. Not recommended on laptops (battery drain).

name: Disable Windows Search Indexing
description: Stops the Windows Search indexing service. Reduces background I/O. Search still works, just slower for the first query.

name: Adjust Visual Effects for Best Performance
description: Disables animations and shadows for maximum UI responsiveness.

name: Disable Transparency Effects
description: Disables Acrylic/blur transparency in the shell — frees GPU resources.

name: Disable Hibernation
description: Removes hiberfil.sys (frees 4–16 GB on C:). Safe if you never use Hibernate or Fast Startup. Reboot required.

name: Disable NTFS 8.3 Short Filenames
description: Stops NTFS generating legacy 8.3 names. Reduces file create overhead on SSDs with many files.

name: Disable NTFS Last Access Timestamp
description: Stops NTFS updating the last-access time on every file read — reduces write I/O on busy directories.

name: Enable Hardware-Accelerated GPU Scheduling (HAGS)
description: GPU manages its own VRAM scheduling — reduces CPU-GPU latency. Requires NVIDIA 10xx+, AMD RX 5000+, or Intel 12th gen+. Reboot required.

name: Disable Memory Page Combining
description: Stops Windows combining identical memory pages in background. Reduces latency spikes caused by page re-splitting on write.

name: Boost Multimedia Scheduling Responsiveness
description: Tells the Multimedia Class Scheduler to dedicate more CPU time to foreground tasks — reduces stutters in audio/video apps.

name: Remove Menu Animation Delay
description: Sets the context menu pop-up delay to 0 ms — menus appear instantly instead of fading in after a short pause.

name: Remove Startup App Launch Delay
description: Eliminates the artificial delay Windows adds before launching startup programs. Apps registered in the startup folder open immediately after login.

name: Disable Microsoft Edge Startup Boost & Preloading
description: Stops Edge from loading in the background at boot and on every new tab. Frees 100–300 MB RAM if you don't use Edge as your primary browser.

name: Keep OS Kernel in RAM (No Paging)
description: Prevents the Windows kernel from being swapped to the pagefile. Improves kernel response time on systems with 8 GB+ RAM.

name: Boost Foreground App CPU Priority
description: Sets Win32PrioritySeparation to favour foreground applications with shorter, variable quanta and maximum boost. Makes the active window feel more responsive.

name: Disable Scheduled Defragmentation
description: Disables the Windows scheduled disk defragmentation task. Not needed on SSDs — defragmenting an SSD causes unnecessary wear with no performance benefit.

name: Ensure TRIM is Enabled for SSD
description: Confirms NTFS TRIM is enabled so the SSD knows which data blocks are unused and can erase them during idle. Required for proper SSD performance over time.

name: Disable Storage Sense
description: Disables Storage Sense which automatically frees disk space by deleting temp files and older downloads. You control cleanup manually.

name: Increase MFT Reserved Zone
description: Increases the NTFS Master File Table reserved zone to reduce fragmentation of frequently accessed files on NTFS volumes.

name: Disable Large System Cache
description: Keeps the file system cache memory-limited. Default Windows behaviour. Only change this if you have specifically benchmarked the large system cache as beneficial for your workload.

name: Optimize File System Cache Size
description: Sets the file system cache to a balanced 512 MB working set. Prevents excessive RAM consumption by the cache on systems with limited memory.

name: Disable Deferred Write Caching
description: Disables the Windows deferred write flusher that batches disk writes. Can improve responsiveness on battery but may risk data loss on power failure.

name: Set Storage I/O Priority to High
description: Gives foreground apps higher priority over background processes for storage I/O. Reduces stutters when background tasks are accessing the disk.

name: Disable Prefetch
description: Disables the Prefetch mechanism that preloads frequently used applications into memory. Reduces disk activity but may slow application launch times on HDDs.

name: Disable Read-Ahead
description: Disables the NTFS read-ahead optimisation that pre-reads sequential file data. Recommended for random-access workloads.

name: Disable All CPU Power Throttling
description: Disables Windows CPPC2 (Collaborative Processor Performance Control) power throttling for all processes. CPU always runs at full rated speed regardless of workload classification.
